message:
feat(cli,session): add session migration command, credential pruning, and pipe-aware print mode

Three related improvements to CLI robustness and session management:

1. Session migration (pi migrate <path> [--dry-run]):
   Converts JSONL v1 session files to the v2 segment format. Supports
   both individual files and directory batch migration. Dry-run mode
   validates the migration (entry count match, hash chain integrity,
   index consistency) without persisting changes. Integrated into the
   conformance fixture runner for test coverage.

2. Stale credential pruning at startup:
   After refreshing OAuth tokens, the auth system now prunes credentials
   that are >7 days past expiry and lack refresh metadata. This prevents
   stale entries from accumulating in auth.json across provider churn.
   Pruned providers are logged at INFO level.

3. Pipe-aware print mode:
   When stdout is a terminal, print mode renders markdown with formatting
   via PiConsole. When piped (e.g., `pi print "..." | grep`), it emits
   plain text via output_final_text to avoid polluting downstream
   consumers with ANSI escape codes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
